---
title: "Machine Learning Classification of Infants' Full-Day Movements"
shorttitle: "Classifying Movements"
author:
  - name: John M. Franchak
    corresponding: true
    orcid: 0000-0002-0751-2864
    email: franchak@ucr.edu
    affiliations:
      - name: University of California, Riverside
        department: Department of Psychology
        address: 900 University Avenue
        city: Riverside
        region: CA
        country: USA
        postal-code: 92521
author-note:
  status-changes: 
    affiliation-change: ~
    deceased: ~
  disclosures:
    study-registration: ~
    data-sharing: ~
    related-report: ~
    conflict-of-interest: ~
    financial-support: ~
    gratitude: ~
    authorship-agreements: ~
abstract: "This document is a template."
impact-statement: ~
keywords: [Keyword 1, Keyword 2, Keyword 3]
bibliography: references.bib
format:
  apaquarto-docx: default
  apaquarto-html: default
  apaquarto-pdf:
    documentmode: man
---

Position and restraint describe opportunities to practice motor skills and to interact with the physical and social world.  

Movement is distributed heterogeneously. Full day is important, but its difficult to study. Video is the gold standard, but full day video is challenging.

LENA is best success in full day recording; transcription is resource intensive, wearable audio is easy to collect, job is needing an algorithm to translate .wav into AWC. Analogous challenge for wearable IMUs. What's been done already. [@AiraksinenRasanen2020].

Chapter focuses on our workflow used in past work focusing on full-day position and restraint [@FranchakTang2024; @KretchEnriques2026; @WangHRouseyHFranchakJM2026].

# Data Collection

Some past work in the lab. Focus here is in the home. Current limits (driving/strollers). 

## Sensor Garment
Interior pockets with closures for safety
Accel and gyro
Number of sensors
Sensor placement 
Maiju's approach; other work w/ chestworn sensor (Greenspan)

## Capturing Ground Truth Video
Synchronization
Amount of data
Coverage of positions (e.g. sitting variations) and mix of freeplay

## Full-Day or Multiple-Day Considerations
Capturing data past video recorded portion
Nap/wear time logging Sensor drift

# Data Processing and Model Training

## Extracting Motion Features
Window size
Features
Accel vs. gyro features (Airaksinen)

## Scoring Ground Truth
Synchronization
Developing coding scheme is what determines output

# Model Evaluation
Point to general refs about binary and multi-class categorization
Project needs dictate what is relevant for evaluation (overall metrics vs. bouts, absolute time vs. relative time, generalization to new exemplars vs. best prediction of current data)

## Training/Testing Partition

Individual models vs. group models and generalization
LOOCV examples 
Sensor CP model composition and size examples

## Evaluation Metrics

Accuracy/kappa
Kaya lab paper

Continuous outcomes (Yao) for prevalence predictions
Bout structure

# Applications

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
